_meta.titus_model: PAR/PXP/PMR (flush face) / PXP-DR/PDR (drop face) -- Perforated Face return diffuser series
_meta.description: Ceiling return, round neck. Supersedes the earlier 50F-based plan (2026-07-23) once this genuinely return-specific cutsheet was found.
_meta.source_cutsheet: perforatedperf_diffusers_2017.pdf, user-supplied from C:\Users\Colin Nolan\Downloads\, page F87 specifically ("PAR, PXP, PMR, PXP-DR, PDR PERFORMANCE DATA", explicitly labeled FLUSH FACE - RETURN / DROP FACE - RETURN)
_meta.why_this_replaced_50F: 50F (Titus specialized grilles cutsheet) only gave ONE CFM/NC row per face size, with no per-neck-diameter breakdown -- nothing to size a variable Duct Diameter against. This Perforated Face return cutsheet has genuine multiple-round-diameter-per-face data for 24x24, making a TMS-style cascade possible there.
_meta.no_deflection_correction_needed: Unlike the 272RL aeroblade sidewall grilles, this is a straight ducted diffuser (not a deflection-blade louvered grille) -- NC values used directly, no +7-style correction, same as the TMS ceiling supply family.
_meta.target_nc: NC < 15, matching the precedent set by SD-1/SD-2 (ceiling supply, TMS) after several rounds of tightening. Not independently re-confirmed by the user for RG-1 specifically after this new cutsheet was found -- proposed and proceeded on absence of objection, flag if this needs revisiting.
_meta.DATA_COVERAGE_GAP_2026-07-24: Round-diameter ("X Dia.") coverage is very uneven across the family's 3 Types: 24x24 face has 6 real diameter options (6"-16"), 12x12 face has only ONE (6" Dia.), and 18x18 has NONE at all in this cutsheet (only a square "18x18 neck (20x20 Face)" substitute in the Other Sizes table, which is a much larger free area than any round option and not really comparable). User's decision: use the real NC-driven cascade where data supports it (24x24 only), and fall back to 600 FPM velocity-sizing (computed from duct area, not tabulated) for 12x12 and 18x18. For 18x18's Sizing Warning ceiling specifically, user explicitly approved an educated-guess use of the 20x20-face substitute row's NC<15 capacity (900 cfm) as the best available reference, despite the neck-geometry mismatch caveat above -- flagged as an approximation, not verified against a true 18x18-round-neck test.
_meta.velocity_sizing_formula: Duct Diameter (in) computed at 600 FPM from area = CFM/600 (sqft), diameter = sqrt(4*area/pi) -- implemented as a lookup against standard round sizes rather than a live sqrt() formula in Revit (safer, matches this project's established IF-cascade pattern rather than untested arithmetic-unit behavior). Cross-validated: computed values at 600fpm exactly match the cutsheet's own 600fpm column for the sizes where real data exists (e.g. 12" diameter -> 471 cfm matches the cutsheet's 12 Dia. row at the 600 fpm column exactly), confirming the math is consistent with Titus's own core-velocity convention.
_meta.family_parameters: Flow (instance), Duct Diameter (instance, formula below), Duct Radius (instance, pre-existing formula Duct Diameter/2, untouched), Diffuser Width/Height (TYPE, discriminator, 12x12/18x18/24x24 -> 1'/1.5'/2'), Sizing Warning (instance, Text, added 2026-07-24), Line length and Total Pressure (instance, pre-existing, untouched, unrelated to sizing).
_meta.verified: 18 boundary test cases (regenerate-and-read, not just formula-compile) across all 3 Types, all pass -- 2026-07-24.

type_12x12.source: 12 x 12 Face, 6 Dia. row only (only published round option for this face)
type_12x12.beyond_118_cfm: No real data -- Duct Diameter continues via 600fpm velocity-sizing (8", 10") but Sizing Warning fires since it exceeds the only verified-quiet data point

velocity_sized_breakpoints.note: 10" is the else/ceiling clause -- capped here since a 12" round neck is already the geometric max that fits a 12x12 face

type_18x18.source: NO direct 18x18 face row exists. Substitute: "18x18 neck (20x20 Face)" from the Other Sizes table -- cfm [675,900,1125,1350,1575,1800,2250,2700,3150], NC [-,11,17,22,26,29,35,40,44]
type_18x18.sizing_warning_note: OVER CAPACITY message explicitly says ESTIMATED to flag the substitute-data caveat to whoever reads it in Revit

type_24x24.source: 24 x 24 Face, all 6 round diameter rows (real NC-driven data)

nc15_breakpoints_ascending.note: final/ceiling tier - also the unconditioned else clause, and the Sizing Warning ceiling for this Type
